MH3U ONLINE BUNDLE - QUICK START
================================

A ready-to-run patched Cemu for playing MH3U online on a self-hosted server. No
Python, no account to create, no Nintendo files, and online is ALREADY switched
on for you. You bring only your own MH3U game dump.

Everything runs from ONE app: MH3U_Online.exe. Double-click it and pick JOIN
(play on someone's server) or HOST (run a server for your friends). It is now
all-in-one - the server is built in, so there is no separate "Host Add-on" to
download anymore.

You connect over an overlay VPN - Tailscale or Radmin - a free app that tunnels
straight to the host's PC over the internet and sidesteps all router/NAT setup.
The host does NOT need to port-forward for this.


HEADS UP: WINDOWS WILL WARN ABOUT THE .EXE FILES
------------------------------------------------
Both MH3U_Online.exe and Cemu_release.exe are custom, unsigned builds (Cemu is the
"mh3u-revival" fork), so Windows Defender / SmartScreen will call them
"unrecognized" or "not commonly downloaded." That's normal for any unsigned tool,
not malware. To run them: "More info" -> "Run anyway" (and/or allow them in
Defender). Want to check them yourself? The full source is on the project page:
    https://github.com/Matt-Wood-23/mh3u-revival

IF ANTIVIRUS BLOCKS OR DELETES MH3U_Online.exe: use the backup
"If antivirus blocks the launcher - JOIN.bat" instead. It's plain text (nothing to
quarantine), does the exact same JOIN setup, and needs no Python. (Hosting still
needs the launcher's HOST tab.)


STEPS
-----
1. Unzip this whole folder anywhere (e.g. C:\MH3U_Online). Keep MH3U_Online.exe,
   Cemu_release.exe, server.exe, "resources", "gameProfiles" and "portable"
   together.

2. Install Tailscale (https://tailscale.com/download) and join the HOST's tailnet:
   install, sign in, accept the host's invite, THEN make sure your client is on the
   HOST's tailnet, not your own (Tailscale auto-makes you a personal one). If the host
   can't see your device: switch to their tailnet in the Tailscale UI and LOG OUT / log
   back in on the app to re-bind. The host gives you their Tailscale IP (a 100.x
   address); confirm you can reach it - `tailscale ping <that IP>` should say "pong".

   PREFER RADMIN VPN? Often simpler: install Radmin VPN (https://www.radmin-vpn.com/),
   click Network -> "Join an existing network", and enter the network NAME + PASSWORD the
   host gives you. Both PCs then show 26.x.x.x IPs; the host gives you THEIR 26.x to use as
   the host IP. Confirm first: `ping <host's 26.x>` should reply.

3. Add your MH3U dump (NOT included - bring your own). You need BOTH:
       MONSTER HUNTER 3 ULTIMATE [Game]   [0005000010118300]   (~6 GB)
       MONSTER HUNTER 3 ULTIMATE [Update] [0005000e10118300]   (~0.2 GB)

   Pick EITHER way:

   (a) Point Cemu at them - EASIEST, no copying:
       Cemu -> File -> "Add games directory" -> pick the folder that CONTAINS those
       two dump folders (the PARENT - e.g. "...\Wii U\Games", NOT a [Game] folder
       itself; Cemu lists games found INSIDE the folder you pick). MH3U then shows
       up in the game list.

   (b) Or copy them into the bundle so it just works:
       put the dump's "code", "content", "meta" folders inside
           portable\mlc01\usr\title\00050000\10118300\
       (and the update's into ...\0005000e\10118300\ if you have it).
       (10118300 is the US title id. EU/PAL game = 10117200, JP "MH3G HD Ver." =
       10104D00 - use YOUR game's id in both the title path here and the save path
       below. JP also needs extra system files - see the JP note in TROUBLESHOOTING
       below.)

   NOTE: the launcher may say "No MH3U game found" - it only checks the copy-in
   location (b). If you used a Game Path (a) and Cemu lists the game, ignore that line.

   Bringing a save from another Cemu? This bundle is a FRESH portable Cemu, so your
   hunter isn't in it yet. IMPORTANT - launch the game ONCE first (step 4) so Cemu
   creates the save folder, then CLOSE it and copy your existing save's contents into:
       portable\mlc01\usr\save\00050000\10118300\user\80000001\
   (That "user\80000001" folder won't exist until that first launch.) Prefer a clean
   start? Just play - a new hunter is made automatically. NOTE: use a save made for
   YOUR game's own region (a US save on the US game, EU on EU).

4. Double-click MH3U_Online.exe and choose:

   JOIN (you're playing on someone else's server):
     - On the Join tab, paste the HOST'S IP (their Tailscale 100.x or Radmin 26.x
       from step 2) and click Save + Play.
     - The first time, the launcher quietly creates your own unique online identity
       (a random NEX PID - nobody else gets the same one) and saves the host IP, then
       starts Cemu. You can change the host IP any time - the field is always editable.

   HOST (you're running the server for friends):
     - On the Host tab, pick which IP your friends will connect to (it auto-suggests
       your Radmin / Tailscale / LAN address) and click Start Server. Give that IP to
       your friends. Use "Host + Play" to start the server AND join it yourself at
       127.0.0.1.

5. Online is ALREADY enabled in this bundle - you do NOT need to touch Cemu's
   account settings. (It comes pre-set to Network Service = Nintendo with a valid
   online account, so the green "online" checkmark is there from the start.) Only if
   that checkmark is ever MISSING (it says "not linked to a NNID or PNID"): CLOSE Cemu
   and run MH3U_Online.exe again -> Join tab -> Save + Play; it re-mints a valid
   identity and the check goes green. (Don't bother setting Network Service by hand:
   it's already on Nintendo, and grayed-out until the account is valid anyway.)

6. Make sure the HOST has their server running and is sitting in a room. Then
   launch MH3U -> main menu -> Network Mode -> enter the SAME Gathering Hall as
   the host -> Enter a Room -> join the host's room.


UPDATES
-------
MH3U_Online.exe checks GitHub for new bundle releases on startup and shows an
"update available" button when there's a newer version. Click it and the launcher
downloads + applies the update in place (your account, save, and game dump are never
touched), then restarts itself. No manual re-download needed.


TROUBLESHOOTING
---------------
(Full page with more fixes: https://github.com/Matt-Wood-23/mh3u-revival/blob/main/docs/TROUBLESHOOTING.md)

- You can share the Gathering Hall fine, but joining a ROOM instantly disconnects =
  the direct player-to-player connection is blocked (halls run on the server; rooms
  are P2P between your PCs). Almost always Windows Firewall on one of the PCs: check
  BOTH PCs for Cemu BLOCK rules (Win+R -> wf.msc -> Inbound Rules; a Block rule
  overrides any Allow rule - delete it), then allow Cemu on BOTH Private and Public
  profiles (VPN adapters like RadminVPN often count as Public). UPnP/your router is
  NOT involved when you're on a VPN.
- MH3U_Online.exe won't open / got deleted by antivirus = use the backup
  "If antivirus blocks the launcher - JOIN.bat" for joining. To host from a machine
  where the launcher is blocked, add an exclusion for MH3U_Online.exe in Defender.
- Launcher says "No MH3U game found" but Cemu lists the game = you used a Game Path
  (step 3a). That message only checks the copy-in folder; ignore it.
- Game not in Cemu's list after "Add games directory" = you probably picked a [Game]
  folder itself. Pick its PARENT folder instead (the one that CONTAINS it).
- "Connected to the internet" then drops back to village = it can't reach the
  host's server. 99% of the time that's the VPN: on Tailscale, confirm you can reach
  the host's 100.x IP (`tailscale ping` -> "pong") and that you're on the host's tailnet
  (step 2); on Radmin, `ping` the host's 26.x IP. Fix that first.
- Host can't see your device on their tailnet = your client is on your OWN tailnet.
  Switch to the host's tailnet in the Tailscale UI, then LOG OUT and back in on the
  app to re-bind. Accepting the invite alone doesn't do it.
- Wrong host IP? Just reopen MH3U_Online.exe -> Join tab, type the new IP, Save + Play.
  (Using the fallback .bat instead? It shows the saved host and gives a 5-second window
  to press C and change it.)
- "Network Service" options look grayed out = NORMAL, not a problem. Cemu only lets you
  CHANGE that setting when no game is running AND the green online check is present - but
  the bundle already ships set to Nintendo, so it works fine while grayed. (Opening
  Settings while MH3U is running locks the whole Account tab - expected.) What matters is
  the GREEN CHECK next to "Online play requirements", not whether the radio is clickable.
  If that check is RED, the status line under it names what's missing. If it says "not
  linked to a NNID or PNID", your account is a blank offline one (Cemu made it before you
  ran the launcher) - CLOSE Cemu and run MH3U_Online.exe again -> Join -> Save + Play; it
  swaps in a valid online identity and the check goes green.
- Game crashes entering Network Mode on an EU/PAL game = the known EUR-build bug, and
  THIS bundle already fixes it (the included Cemu + the host's server handle it). Still
  crashing? You're on an older bundle/Cemu - re-download the current one. (Separately, an
  EU/JP save on a US game or vice-versa can misbehave - use a save made for your game's
  own region.)
- JP game ("MH3G HD Ver.", title id 10104D00) needs extra system files on ANY Cemu:
  real snd_user.rpl + snduser2.rpl in portable\cafeLibs\, plus the Japanese system
  files (fonts) merged into portable\mlc01 (get both via CemuMegaDownloader:
  https://github.com/Xpl0itU/CemuMegaDownloader). A JP black screen at boot is fixed
  in THIS bundle's Cemu (older bundles: also copy swkbd.rpl from the JP game's own
  "code" folder into portable\cafeLibs\). Naming your hunter? The JP keyboard shows
  on the GAMEPAD screen - hold Tab (or Ctrl+Tab) in Cemu to see it.
- Windows blocked Cemu_release.exe? See "HEADS UP" above - "More info" -> "Run anyway".
- Do NOT run "Launch with GDB stub" - just use the launcher / Cemu_release.exe.
- Portable: all data lives in "portable". Delete the folder to remove everything.
- No Nintendo files are included. otp/seeprom are zeros and the "certs" are
  4-byte empty stubs - they only satisfy Cemu's file-existence check, because the
  patched build sends the real connection to the host's server, not Nintendo.


HOSTING, IN ONE LINE
--------------------
Open MH3U_Online.exe -> Host tab -> Start Server, and tell your friends the IP it
shows. That's it - server.exe is already in this folder, so there is nothing extra
to install. To host and play on the same PC, use "Host + Play" (joins at 127.0.0.1).
